class.name: PainterlyMaterial
class.brief_description: Resource that defines painterly and non-photorealistic shading styles for Gaussian splats.
class.description: PainterlyMaterial controls how Gaussian splats are stylized during rendering. It supports multiple shading styles (realistic, cel, painterly, Gooch), palette-based color quantization, brush stroke modulation, and configurable lighting with rim, specular, and shadow parameters. Enable individual feature groups with [member palette_quantization_enabled], [member brush_modulation_enabled], and [member lighting_stylization_enabled]. Use [member style_preset] to apply a predefined configuration, or set [member shading_style] and individual parameters for full control. Palette textures and noise lookup tables can be assigned to drive color quantization and brush variation. A stroke density curve allows spatially varying brush density across the rendered scene.

method.name: add_palette_texture
method.description: Appends a palette texture to the palette texture list.

method.name: remove_palette_texture
method.description: Removes the palette texture at the given index.

method.name: get_palette_texture_count
method.description: Returns the number of palette textures currently assigned.

method.name: add_noise_lut
method.description: Appends a noise lookup table texture.

method.name: remove_noise_lut
method.description: Removes the noise lookup table texture at the given index.

method.name: get_noise_lut_count
method.description: Returns the number of noise lookup table textures currently assigned.

method.name: get_stroke_density_lut
method.description: Returns the baked stroke density lookup table computed from [member stroke_density_curve]. The array length matches [member stroke_density_resolution].

method.name: get_shader_define_strings
method.description: Returns shader preprocessor define strings reflecting the current material configuration, for use by the rendering pipeline.

method.name: has_required_resources
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if all resources needed by the enabled feature set are assigned (e.g., palette textures when quantization is on).

method.name: get_missing_resources
method.description: Returns a list of human-readable descriptions of resources that are needed but not yet assigned.

method.name: serialize
method.description: Serializes the full material state to a [Dictionary] for storage or network transfer.

method.name: deserialize
method.description: Restores the material state from a [Dictionary] produced by [method serialize].

members.member: Array of [Texture2D] resources used as color palettes for quantization.
Array of [Texture2D] resources used as noise lookup tables for brush variation.
A [Curve] that maps normalized distance to stroke density, baked into a lookup table at [member stroke_density_resolution].
Resolution of the baked stroke density lookup table. Higher values provide finer gradation.
Multiplier applied to the stroke density curve output.
When enabled, splat colors are quantized toward the assigned palette colors.
When enabled, splat shapes are modulated by brush parameters to produce painterly stroke effects.
When enabled, stylized lighting (cel shading, Gooch, rim) is applied to the splats.
The color palette used for quantization. Supports up to 8 colors.
Controls how strongly splat colors are pulled toward the nearest palette color. Range [code]0.0[/code] (no effect) to [code]1.0[/code] (full snap).
Amount of noise added during palette quantization to break up uniform regions.
When enabled, the original luminance is preserved during palette quantization so that depth cues are not lost.
The active shading style. See [enum ShadingStyle] for options.
Applies a predefined configuration to all material parameters. See [enum StylePreset].
Number of discrete shading bands for cel shading. Range [code]1[/code] to [code]16[/code].
Controls the softness of transitions between cel shading bands. Range [code]0.0[/code] (hard) to [code]1.0[/code] (smooth).
Strength of the painterly color mixing effect. Range [code]0.0[/code] to [code]1.0[/code].
How much the brush texture affects the final splat appearance. Range [code]0.0[/code] to [code]1.0[/code].
Scale multiplier for the brush stroke effect.
Softness of brush stroke edges.
Anisotropy ratio for brush strokes. Values above [code]1.0[/code] elongate strokes.
Random rotation variation applied to each brush stroke. Range [code]0.0[/code] to [code]1.0[/code].
Amount of shape noise applied to brush strokes to produce organic variation.
Color of the primary directional light used in stylized lighting.
Ambient fill light color for stylized lighting.
Direction vector of the primary light source used in stylized lighting calculations.
Strength of diffuse lighting contribution.
Strength of specular highlight contribution.
Strength of rim lighting effect along silhouette edges.
Exponent controlling specular highlight sharpness.
Exponent controlling rim lighting falloff.
Color of the rim lighting effect.
Color tint applied to shadowed regions in stylized lighting.
Color tint applied to highlighted regions in stylized lighting.
White point color temperature in Kelvin. Range [code]1000[/code] to [code]20000[/code].
Strength of the color temperature adjustment effect.
Controls temporal filtering strength for reducing frame-to-frame flickering in stylized shading.
Cool tone mix factor for Gooch shading style.
Warm tone mix factor for Gooch shading style.
Overall intensity multiplier for all stylized lighting contributions.

constants.constant: Standard physically-based shading with no stylization.
Cel (toon) shading with discrete light bands controlled by [member cel_band_count].
Painterly shading that simulates hand-painted surfaces with color mixing.
Gooch cool-to-warm shading often used in technical illustration.
No preset applied; all parameters are manually configured.
Preset that configures parameters for a realistic look with minimal stylization.
Preset that configures cel shading with strong bands and outline-like rim lighting.
Preset that configures a painterly look with brush modulation and palette influence.
Preset that configures Gooch-style shading suited for technical or scientific visualization.
